mnemonics.title: Reference Rules RAP
mnemonics.content:
R - References hold addresses, not values
A - Assignment copies the address, not the object
P - Primitive values are copied directly

acronyms.usage: Manages memory allocation and garbage collection for objects in heap
Thrown when trying to access members of a null reference
Paradigm where objects and references are fundamental concepts

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box (heap) for object, an arrow from variable box (stack) pointing to the object box. Assignment adds another arrow from second variable to same object.
Think of two TV remote controls pointing to the same TV. Pressing buttons on either remote controls the same TV.
Draw a disconnected arrow or an 'X' showing the reference doesn't point anywhere.
Shallow: Draw object with nested object inside - copy has same nested object pointer. Deep: Draw completely separate nested object in the copy.

analogies.analogy: Like a house address written on a paper. The paper (reference) is not the house (object), but tells you where the house is located.
Like giving your friend the same address paper - you both now know where the same house is, but there's only one house.
Think of heap as a large parking lot where cars (objects) are parked. References are like parking ticket numbers telling you where your car is.
Like a cleaning crew that collects houses (objects) that no one has addresses (references) to anymore.
